Yes, the Company’s “Open Door” communication policy ensures that employees and workers are able to voice their concerns without fear of reprisal. They are encouraged to promptly discuss any issues with their immediate manager. If a problem is not resolved or further clarification is needed, they can request a meeting with their Business Unit (BU) Head or Human Resource (HR) Head. If a concern remains unresolved or requires confidentiality, they may contact the Employee Grievance Cell in writing to grievance@aurionpro.com. Further the POSH Policy (Intranet) and Whistle Blower Policy  (https://www.aurionpro.com/archive/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/whistle-blower-vigil-mechanism-policy.pdf) also provide adequate mechanisms for redressal to the aggrieved employee or worker. Individuals may report violations of the Code of Conduct by sending a mail to  conduct_ethics@aurionpro.com or submitting a written complaint to the Chairman of the Conduct & Ethics Committee. Anonymous complaints are also accepted and handled with strict confidentiality and care.

Customers are a very critical part of our value chain. For the customers we have formal mechanisms in place for handling complaints and feedback, alongside an institutional framework – the Steering Committee, which is composed of representatives from both the company and customers, to address and resolve any grievances. The customers can contact us in case of grievances at:  info@aurionpro.com
Contracts with value chain partners incorporate clear communication channels and a structured grievance redressal mechanism.

With the continued growth of the Company and its subsidiaries in delivering products and services through digital platforms, managing the dual priorities of data-driven innovation and customer privacy has become increasingly complex. The reliance on various forms of user data including personal, demographic, behavioural, and content-related information to tailor experiences and support business growth may lead to heightened oversight from global regulatory bodies. Furthermore, offering cloud-based solutions and IT infrastructure introduces additional concerns, particularly around the possibility of government entities accessing user data, potentially leading to infringements of individual freedom and privacy rights.

In response to these challenges, the Company and its subsidiaries are committed to adopting comprehensive data governance frameworks that emphasize regulatory compliance and the safeguarding of user privacy. Proactive oversight in this domain is essential to preserving stakeholder confidence and supporting long-term business resilience.

The Company and its subsidiaries have strategically invested in safeguarding their intellectual assets, including securing trademarks, copyrights, and other key forms of IP that underpin innovation and competitive advantage. Our ability to operate effectively depends on leveraging proprietary technologies while avoiding infringement on third-party rights. Nonetheless, there remains a risk of legal disputes arising from alleged violations of intellectual property or copyright laws. Unfavourable outcomes in such matters whether regulatory or judicial could lead to extended legal proceedings, substantial financial liabilities, and erosion of market influence. Should the core technologies or market leadership be legally contested, the resulting impact on revenue generation and strategic positioning could be significant.

Recognizing the importance of mitigating intellectual property risks, the Company and its subsidiaries have instituted a robust framework to safeguard proprietary innovations. This includes well-defined protocols for handling confidential and sensitive information related to intellectual assets. When acquiring intellectual property, we undertake rigorous due diligence and, where appropriate, secure indemnification from third-party developers. The agreements with employees, workers and external developers are carefully structured to ensure the assignment of rights for all IP created in the course of engagement, operating under a work-for-hire model. Additionally, the contractual arrangements with clients and vendors are designed with strong protective clauses to uphold our IP rights, supported by enforceable remedies and indemnity provisions in case of violations.
Managing Systemic Risks from Technology Disruptions

The Company and its subsidiaries understand the importance of proactively addressing systemic risks linked to technological disruption, especially in an industry heavily dependent on digital systems. Accelerating innovation, escalating cybersecurity threats, and the possibility of technical failures present tangible risks, including operational interruptions, compromised data integrity, and financial losses. Given our role in delivering cybersecurity, digital transformation, and enterprise solutions, any significant disruption in our technology ecosystem could trigger downstream effects - impacting business continuity not only internally but also across our client network and stakeholder community.

To mitigate the impact of potential technology disruptions, the Company and its subsidiaries have established a multi-layered approach on resilience and preparedness. This includes the deployment of advanced cybersecurity measures, real-time infrastructure monitoring, and frequent upgrades to address emerging threats. A detailed business continuity framework  ensures rapid response and recovery in the face of unforeseen incidents. In addition, ongoing risk evaluations and scenario planning exercises enable the organization to anticipate challenges and maintain consistent service delivery, reinforcing trust among clients and stakeholders.

Placing customer welfare at the forefront presents a strategic opportunity for the Company and its subsidiaries to strengthen market differentiation and cultivate enduring client relationships. By embedding client satisfaction into the core of solution development, the company and its subsidiaries strive to deliver experiences that consistently surpass expectations. This customer-centric focus not only reinforces loyalty but also aligns with a broader global shift toward ethical, transparent, and user-first business models. In turn, this approach enhances brand credibility, supports sustainable growth and contributes meaningfully to long-term revenue generation.

The Company and its subsidiaries recognise that retaining top talent is essential to sustaining excellence and driving innovation. To this end, it cultivates a work culture rooted in support, engagement, and professional growth. Their commitment to employee satisfaction not only fosters loyalty but also ensures consistent service quality and operational stability. Talent acquisition and retention strategies are reinforced through targeted initiatives that nurture a positive and inclusive workplace environment. A well-defined Performance Management System strengthens this approach, enabling transparent assessments and ongoing skill development to help employees thrive and advance within the organization.

Aurionpro is committed to embedding sustainability into its growth journey, guided by the 17 UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Our sustainability focus is built on three pillars: reducing our carbon footprint, promoting responsible practices across our value chain, and maintaining transparent governance standards.

We have set bold targets, including a commitment to significantly cut greenhouse gas emissions and achieve net-zero emissions by 2045. Sustainability is embedded across our IP-led product ecosystem and integrated into our core business models. At Aurionpro, we recognize that true progress is measured across People, Planet, and Profit.

As we chart our future course, we remain committed to deepening community engagement and aligning our operations with global sustainability benchmarks.

On 29th September, 2024 Dr. Mahendra Mehta retired as Non-Executive Independent Director. Due to such retirement, the composition of Board of Directors was not in compliance with the SEBI LODR Regulations, 2015. The Company paid the consolidated fine amount to BSE and NSE for two quarters, that is, Rs. 7,31,600. However, post appointment of Mr. Ajay Kumar Choudhary on 11th November 2024, the composition of the Board is now in compliance with the requirement.

Yes, the Code of Conduct for Directors and Senior Managerial Personnel (https://www.Aurionpro.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/code-of-conduct.pdf) and Code of Conduct (Intranet) outline clear provisions for managing conflicts of interest. The Codes establish disclosure mechanisms for certain transactions which may result in actual or potential conflict of interest. Employees and Directors are required to avoid any business, relationship, or activity that could create an actual or potential conflict of interest with the Company and its subsidiaries. Any unavoidable related party transaction is to be fully disclosed to the Board or Compliance Officer. Directors are also expected to disclose their interests in company transactions and seek consent before engaging in any activity that might prejudice the Company’s interests. This ensures ethical conduct and transparency in decision-making.

Essential 4 -   The Company and its subsidiaries are firmly committed to maintaining the highest standards of integrity and ethical conduct. While it has not adopted a standalone Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption (ABAC) policy, its comprehensive Code of Conduct (Intranet), Code of Conduct for Directors & Senior Management (https://www.aurionpro.com/archive/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/code-of-conduct.pdf) include a specific clauses that prohibits bribery and corruption. This clause serves as a key ethical guideline for all employees, directors and senior management, reinforcing its commitment to transparency and integrity in all operations.

The Code strictly prohibits any form of bribery, corruption, or facilitation payments in the conduct of the Company’s business. Employees, Directors and Senior Managers shall neither offer nor accept, directly or indirectly, any illegal payments, gifts, donations, hospitality, or other benefits intended to secure business or gain any unfair advantage. Only nominal gifts of a customary and commemorative nature, given or received in good faith for special occasions, are permitted. Any act or attempt of bribery or corrupt practice will be treated as a serious breach of the Code and will invite appropriate disciplinary and legal action.

Yes, issues concerning permanent employees and workers - such as department, project, or location changes are handled by HR Business Partners (HRBPs). For other than permanent employees, grievance redressal is managed by the Parent Company, Aurionpro Solutions Limited. In both cases, a structured and fair process ensures prompt resolution of concerns.

The Company’s Code of Conduct and Employee Policy Manual (available on the Intranet) provide dedicated mechanisms for securely and confidentially reporting grievances. Employees are informed that if their concerns remained unresolved, they could approach the Employee Grievance Cell at grievance@aurionpro.com. These mechanisms include provisions for addressing harassment, discrimination, and unethical practices. 

In compliance with the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition & Redressal) Act, 2013, the Company has constituted an Internal Complaints Committee (IC) to handle such matters. These safeguards apply to employees and those engaged through contractual agreements.

Individuals may also report violations of the Code of Conduct by sending a mail to  conduct_ethics@aurionpro.com or submitting a written complaint to the Chairman of the Conduct & Ethics Committee. Anonymous complaints are also accepted and handled with strict confidentiality and care.

The Company is committed to maintaining a safe and healthy work environment for all employees. While the nature of our operations presents minimal exposure to significant hazards, it adheres to established standard operational protocols to ensure workplace safety. Although formalized processes for routine hazard identification and risk assessment are not currently in place, any potential risks that arise are promptly addressed to safeguard the well-being of the workforce.

As per the Company’s Code of Conduct (available on the Intranet), the Company is committed to maintaining a safe and secure work environment in compliance with applicable health and safety standards. The use of drugs or alcohol at the workplace is strictly prohibited. Employees are expected to work safely and responsibly, adhering to all workplace health and safety procedures. They are also required to promptly report any unsafe conditions to their reporting manager or HR, ensuring a culture of accountability and safety. The employees and workers are given training on different subject matter like prevention of sexual harassment at workplace. Further, the employees are given training on time and stress management. 

The Company also maintains a safe and healthy workplace through a comprehensive framework of measures. These include regular safety training, the implementation of occupational health and safety policies, the provision of appropriate safety infrastructure, a structured grievance redressal mechanism and coverage under ESIC and insurance schemes. Collectively, these initiatives foster a secure, supportive, and health-conscious work environment for all personnel.

The stakeholder identification process of the Company and its subsidiaries is structured around evaluating and rating stakeholders against five critical parameters. These include individuals or entities who:
a.	Have the ability to grant or withdraw our social license to operate,
b.	Influence other internal stakeholders,
c.	Possess direct financial dependence,
d.	Exhibit indirect financial dependence,
e.	Are affected by non-financial risks or impairments stemming from our operations.

To ensure a comprehensive assessment, Department Heads and members of the leadership are tasked with rating these groups based on their level of influence and dependence on our business.
